segments.text: Then the Lord spoke to Job out of the storm.
He said:
Who is this that obscures my plans with words without knowledge?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Brace yourself like a man; I will question you, and you shall answer me.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Where were you when I laid the earth's foundation? Tell me, if you understand.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who marked off its dimensions? Surely you know! Who stretched a measuring line across it?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: On what were its footings set, or who laid its cornerstone—
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: while the morning stars sang together and all the angels shouted for joy?
Who shut up the sea behind doors when it burst forth from the womb,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when I made the clouds its garment and wrapped it in thick darkness,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when I fixed limits for it and set its doors and bars in place,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when I said, 'This far you may come and no farther; here is where your proud waves halt'?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Have you ever given orders to the morning, or shown the dawn its place,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: that it might take the earth by the edges and shake the wicked out of it?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The earth takes shape like clay under a seal; its features stand out like those of a garment.
The wicked are denied their light, and their upraised arm is broken.
Have you journeyed to the springs of the sea or walked in the recesses of the deep?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Have the gates of death been shown to you? Have you seen the gates of the deepest darkness?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Have you comprehended the vast expanses of the earth? Tell me, if you know all this.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: What is the way to the abode of light? And where does darkness reside?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you take them to their places? Do you know the paths to their dwellings?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Surely you know, for you were already born! You have lived so many years!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Have you entered the storehouses of the snow or seen the storehouses of the hail,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: which I reserve for times of trouble, for days of war and battle?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: What is the way to the place where the lightning is dispersed, or the place where the east winds are scattered over the earth?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who cuts a channel for the torrents of rain, and a path for the thunderstorm,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: to water a land where no one lives, an uninhabited desert,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: to satisfy a desolate wasteland and make it sprout with grass?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Does the rain have a father? Who fathers the drops of dew?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: From whose womb comes the ice? Who gives birth to the frost from the heavens
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when the waters become hard as stone, when the surface of the deep is frozen?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades? Can you loosen Orion's belt?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you bring forth the constellations in their seasons or lead out the Bear with its cubs?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do you know the laws of the heavens? Can you set up God's dominion over the earth?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you raise your voice to the clouds and cover yourself with a flood of water?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do you send the lightning bolts on their way? Do they report to you, 'Here we are'?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who gives the ibis wisdom or gives the rooster understanding?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who has the wisdom to count the clouds? Who can tip over the water jars of the heavens
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when the dust becomes hard and the clods of earth stick together?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do you hunt the prey for the lioness and satisfy the hunger of the lions
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when they crouch in their dens or lie in wait in a thicket?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who provides food for the raven when its young cry out to God and wander about for lack of food?
segments.speaker: The LORD
